by_eval.notes: NEW skill catches unnecessary exports and frames DI as boundary violation. Neither skill flagged LoD on idiomatic Go struct access (correct behavior — not a real violation).
NEW skill catches DI anti-pattern framed as architectural boundary violation. OLD skill missed it entirely.
Zero false positives for both skill versions. Both correctly identified placeholder generateID() and nil-notifier risk without flagging clean DI patterns.

conclusion: New skill improves boundary violation detection by +12.5% overall (93.75% vs 81.25%) with zero regression on false positives. Key wins: unnecessary exports detection, DI anti-pattern framing, test boundary violations. The single missed expectation (LoD on Go struct access) is a test calibration issue, not a skill deficiency.
